en.extract:
The Sumba eclectus, or Cornelia's eclectus (Eclectus cornelia) is a parrot species which is native to Sumba. Also larger than the Moluccan eclectus, the male is a paler shade of green overall and has a bluer tail. The female has an all red plumage, except for the primaries which are a dark royal blue, and can be differentiated from the Tanimbar eclectus by the lack of yellow to her tail.


Aviculture
The Sumba eclectus can be found in zoos and bird parks in Spain and Germany, although it is uncommon in wider aviculture.

The '''Sumba eclectus''',<ref>{{cite book |last=Steadman |first=David William |year=2006 |title=Extinction and Biogeography of Tropical Pacific Birds |publisher=University of Chicago Press |page=525 |isbn=0-226-77142-3}}</ref> or '''Cornelia's eclectus'''<ref name="taylor">{{cite web |last1=Taylor |first1=Graham |title=Cornelia's Eclectus (Eclectus roratus cornelia) |url=https://eclectus-parrots.com/subspecies-info-cornelias-eclectus/ |access-date=5 February 2023}}</ref> ('''''Eclectus cornelia''''') is a parrot species which is native to [[Sumba]]. Also larger than the [[Moluccan eclectus]], the male is a paler shade of green overall and has a bluer tail. The female has an all red plumage, except for the [[Primary feather|primaries]] which are a dark royal blue,<ref name=Forshaw78>{{cite book| author=Forshaw, Joseph M.| author-link=Joseph Forshaw| author2=Cooper, William T.| name-list-style=amp|year=1978| title=Parrots of the World| edition=2nd|publisher=Landsdowne Editions| location=Melbourne| isbn=0-7018-0690-7|pages=202–07}}</ref> and can be differentiated from the [[Tanimbar eclectus]] by the lack of yellow to her tail.<ref name="taylor" />

==Aviculture==
The Sumba eclectus can be found in zoos and bird parks in Spain and Germany, although it is uncommon in wider aviculture.<ref name="taylor" />
